summary: Comprehensive testing of Today Task application with Service Worker push notifications feature. All backend tests pass (18/18). All frontend features working correctly including Service Worker registration, notification permission banner, task CRUD with reminders, filters, sorting, themes, and premium features.
passed_tests: Backend: User registration with valid data
Backend: Login with valid admin credentials
Backend: Login rejection with invalid password (401)
Backend: Login rejection for non-existent user (401)
Backend: /auth/me returns user data when authenticated
Backend: User preferences update and persistence
Backend: Brute force protection (429 after 5 failed attempts)
Frontend: Task creation with date/time picker
Frontend: Theme switching (Claro, Oscuro, Azul, Verde)
Frontend: Notification dropdown with all options (Ambas, Solo alarma, Solo notificación, Ninguna)
Frontend: Logout redirects to auth page
Frontend: Premium user - Pro button hidden
Frontend: Premium user - Typography unlocked (Clásica, Burbuja, Dibujo)
Frontend: Premium user - Alarms unlocked (4 options)
Frontend: Free user - Typography locked with 'Desbloquear' button
Frontend: Free user - Alarms locked with 'Desbloquear' button
Frontend: In-app notification toast fires for reminders
critical_code_review_comments: Service Worker implementation is solid with proper install/activate lifecycle
Task sync to SW uses sendTasksToSW on task changes - good pattern
Fallback reminder system in TaskApp.js for when SW is not available
Notification permission banner correctly shows when permission is not granted
seed_data_creation: Admin user seeded on startup with premium status. Demo user created during testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All features working correctly. Service Worker is properly registered and activated. Notification permission banner shows correctly in test environment (permission is 'denied' by default in Playwright). The in-app reminder toast fires correctly for tasks with reminders. Previous brute force datetime bug has been fixed. Test fixtures now use unique random suffixes to avoid email collisions.
